message:
feat(core): refactor col_map to 3-tuple and add ambiguous column detection

Extend the column map type from `(String, String)` to `(String, String, bool)`
throughout the query engine to carry per-column metadata (the third field tracks
USING-clause provenance). This change touches every function that builds,
passes, or pattern-matches against col_map entries — approximately 40 call
sites in connection.rs.

Key behavioral changes:
- `find_col_in_map` now accepts an optional `using_skip_indices: Option<&HashSet<usize>>`
  parameter. When provided, USING-clause columns from the right side of a join
  are skipped during unqualified name resolution, preventing false matches.
- Unqualified column lookups now detect ambiguity: if two or more non-skipped
  entries match, `FrankenError::AmbiguousColumn` is returned instead of silently
  picking the first match. This aligns with SQLite's behavior on queries like
  `SELECT id FROM t1 JOIN t2` where both tables define `id`.
- Wire datetime functions (strftime, date, time, datetime, julianday,
  unixepoch, timediff) into `eval_scalar_fn` via `fsqlite_func::ScalarFunction`
  trait dispatch, enabling these functions in the connection eval path that was
  previously only reachable through the VDBE.

All downstream callers (trigger evaluation, HAVING predicates, GROUP BY
resolution, window functions, join evaluation, outer-ref substitution,
scalar projection, affinity coercion) are updated to the new 3-tuple
signature with no functional regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
